title: cassipourea subsessilis
intro: Cassipourea subsessilis, also known as the African dogwood, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Cassipourea subsessilis are said to include wound healing, fever reduction, and the treatment of various infections, including urinary tract infections. Herbal preparations of Cassipourea subsessilis can be made by infusing the leaves and bark in water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in the form of ointments or capsules.
